## Summary

File upload via POST `/api/manuscripts` was failing with "Failed to create manuscript" because the route was inserting a `content` field that does not exist in the `manuscripts` table schema.

**Root cause:** The insert included `content: extractedText`, but the `manuscripts` table has no `content` column — the full text is already stored as a `data:text/plain` URI in `file_url`, which is how `processor.ts` retrieves it downstream.

**Fix:**
1. Remove `content: extractedText` from the manuscripts insert — matches the working pattern used by `/api/evaluate` and `/api/jobs`
2. Improve frontend error surfacing: show backend `details` alongside the generic error message so authors see the actual failure reason

**Note:** The `file_url` data URI pattern is used by all other manuscript-creating routes (`/api/evaluate`, `/api/jobs`, `/api/admin/proof/jobs`) and is the canonical text storage mechanism read by `processor.ts` via `decodeURIComponent`. No change to that pattern.

## Scope

**Touches:** `app/api/manuscripts/route.ts` (remove `content` from insert), `components/evaluation/ManuscriptSubmissionForm.jsx` (error message includes details)

**Does NOT touch:** evaluation pipeline, scoring, file extraction logic, Supabase schema, any other manuscript-creating routes (they already work correctly)

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- Minimal risk: only removes a field that was causing the insert to fail
- No behavioral change for existing manuscripts — they were never created via this route (it was broken)
- The `data:text/plain` URI in `file_url` can be large for full novels; this is an existing pattern across all routes, not introduced here

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed — upload route only, no pipeline or evaluation logic changes
